title: Edison International: Grid Modernization Creating Long-Term Growth
text: Edison International pays out a strong dividend and has outperformed the S&P 500 in the past 10Y. Grid modernization has created efficiencies that will expand margins and improve the customer experience.

title: These 4 Dividends Are Flexing Their Relative Muscles
text: A select group of income heroes are displaying notable “relative strength” right now.

title: 2 Picks To Defy Putin, Xi, And J. Powell
text: Profit motives and innovation drive the markets forward, not politics and world events. The market has consistently operated amidst looming fears; from Zero Covid in China, war risk with Russia, and inflation and higher interest rates from Jerome Powell.

title: EIX vs. PEG: Which Stock Is the Better Value Option?
text: Investors looking for stocks in the Utility - Electric Power sector might want to consider either Edison International (EIX) or PSEG (PEG). But which of these two stocks offers value investors a better bang for their buck right now?

title: Active ETF Flows Strong in 2023 — Why?
text: With $28 billion of net inflows in the first four months of 2023, according to VettaFi LOGICLY data, active ETF investments continue to gain traction with advisors and end clients. Two of the firms benefiting from the trend are Dimensional Funds and Capital Group.

title: Edison International (EIX) Q1 Earnings Surpass Estimates
text: Edison International's (EIX) first-quarter 2023 adjusted earnings beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 4.8%. Meanwhile, revenues are almost in line with the year-ago quarter's figure.

title: Edison International (EIX) Surpasses Q1 Earnings Estimates
text: Edison International (EIX) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.09 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.04 per share. This compares to earnings of $1.07 per share a year ago.
